key_departures.impact: Three-year starting left guard who earned a Pro Bowl in 2024 and posted a 76.4 pass-blocking PFF grade (7th among guards) in 2025. Departed on a 3yr/$31.5M deal to Arizona. Leaves Pittsburgh with a significant hole at LG with no obvious replacement currently on roster. Spencer Anderson is the leading internal option but is not a proven starter.

Team MVP in 2025 with 1,023 scrimmage yards, 73 receptions, and 8 touchdowns. Was Aaron Rodgers' primary check-down option and pass-catching back. Departs for Tampa Bay on a 2yr/$14M deal. The loss is significant — Gainwell was a dual-threat back whose receiving ability is not replicated by Jaylen Warren or Kaleb Johnson.

Started as CB2 after taking over for Darius Slay in 2025. Had a breakout season but departed to Minnesota on a modest 2yr/$8.5M deal. Departure is partially offset by Dean signing and Samuel re-signing, but was a legitimate starter.

Released on March 4, saving $7M in cap space. Came over in the Jalen Ramsey trade but averaged only 5.8 yards per reception and 4.1 yards per target in 2025. Release affirms commitment to Pat Freiermuth and Darnell Washington as the TE1/TE2 duo. TE depth is now thin.

Finished 2025 with 3,220 yards, 24 TDs, 7 INTs in a 10-7 season. Has not committed to returning. Is openly non-committal about his NFL future as of March 11. Steelers have Will Howard (6th-round 2025 pick, zero NFL snaps) and Mason Rudolph under contract as fallback options. QB remains the team's most urgent unresolved need entering the draft.

signings.trade_details: Late-round pick swap with Colts (reported as late-round picks exchanged in both directions)

contract.guaranteed: TBD — reported as significant given extension structure

signings.analysis: Acquired via trade from Indianapolis in a late-round pick swap, then immediately signed to a 3yr/$59M extension at $19.67M AAV — squarely in starter tier for WR. Pittman has had at least 65 receptions in each of the past five seasons. He slides in as WR2 alongside DK Metcalf. The 3-year contract with meaningful guarantees signals a genuine long-term investment. WR2 need is fully addressed for the foreseeable future. WR depth (WR3) still exists as a minor need.

contract.guaranteed: TBD — reported as significant; deal noted as second-largest FA contract in Steelers history

signings.analysis: 3yr/$36.75M deal makes this a genuine long-term investment — AAV of $12.25M is solidly in CB starter tier. Dean had 3 INTs in 14 starts in 2025 and was considered one of the top CB free agents on the market. He slots in as CB2 alongside Joey Porter Jr., filling the hole left by James Pierre. With Samuel also re-signed to provide depth, CB is no longer a pressing need. Dean's age (turns 30 during 2026 season) and injury history (12 games missed in last 3 seasons) are mild risk flags but the 3-year deal signals conviction.

2yr/$12.25M replaces the volume of Kenneth Gainwell at RB but NOT the same skillset. Dowdle is a more pure, physical runner with back-to-back 1,000-yard seasons; Gainwell was Pittsburgh's primary pass-catching/check-down back. Dowdle has familiarity with McCarthy's scheme from his Cowboys tenure. He pairs with Jaylen Warren to form a solid rushing committee. However, the Steelers still lack a true receiving back, which was Gainwell's greatest value. The pass-catching void at RB is partially unaddressed — partially mitigated by Dowdle's adequate receiving profile but not fully resolved. RB need drops from urgent to moderate.

Contract restructure/extension adds one year to his deal, keeping him through 2027 and freeing $5.5M in 2026 cap space. Heyward was a 2nd-team All-Pro in 2025 with 78 tackles and 3.5 sacks. At $16.25M guaranteed and $18M new-money APY, this is the largest contract ever given to an NFL defensive player aged 36+. This was a retention of an existing starter — IDL was not a need beforehand and remains a strength. Heyward, Keeanu Benton, and Derrick Harmon form a strong starting IDL group.

One-year, $4M deal is a depth/prove-it signing. Samuel started 3 of 6 games after joining mid-season 2025 following spinal fusion surgery recovery. With Dean signing as the true CB2 investment, Samuel provides slot depth and competition. With a full offseason under his belt he could develop into a legitimate CB3 or even push for more starts. The 1-year structure is a low-commitment bet on his health and upside. Doesn't address any need on its own but complements the Dean signing well.

contract.guaranteed: TBD — low, consistent with depth deal

signings.analysis: 2yr/$5M is a depth/special teams deal. Holcomb slots in as LB3 behind Patrick Queen and Payton Wilson. LB was not a need entering free agency and this re-signing maintains depth. Low cap hit has minimal roster impact. The 2-year structure gives slight continuity but the AAV is below starter level — this is a rotational/STs re-signing.

contract.total_value: TBD — reported as likely near minimum
contract.aav: TBD — estimated ~$1.5-2M based on RFA tender context

signings.analysis: One-year deal to retain a rotational IDL piece who appeared in 12 games in 2025 with 9 tackles and a sack. This is a depth signing — Otomewo is not a starter. IDL is a strength and this merely preserves roster depth behind Heyward, Benton, and Harmon. No need impact.

contract.total_value: TBD — likely cheap, below prior $3M/yr deal

signings.analysis: Johnston originally signed a 3yr/$9M deal with Pittsburgh in 2024 but missed nearly all of that season with a knee injury. He then lost a 2025 camp battle to Corliss Waitman, bounced to Buffalo (injured again) and the Giants. This 1-year deal is low-cost competition at punter. Waitman is now an untended RFA whose fate is uncertain. Johnston adds a proven option but his recent injury history and competition with Waitman leave the position unsettled.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Aaron Rodgers remains unsigned and non-committal as of March 11. Will Howard (6th-round rookie with zero NFL snaps) and Mason Rudolph are the only QBs under contract. No QB signing has been made in free agency. This remains the team's single most urgent need — and arguably the most uncertain situation in the entire league. Tier 1 critical need unchanged, possibly elevated. Draft implications are massive.

Isaac Seumalo (3yr/$31.5M to Cardinals) was the starting left guard and one of the better pass-blocking guards in the NFL (76.4 PFF grade, 7th among guards). No replacement has been signed. The market has thinned rapidly — Alijah Vera-Tucker (top option) signed with New England. Spencer Anderson is the leading internal option but is unproven as a full-time starter. This need has escalated from tier 2 to tier 1. Left guard is a genuine hole requiring either a free agent signing or early draft capital.

Broderick Jones (LT) underwent neck fusion surgery in December 2025 and his status for Week 1 is uncertain. His 2026 availability is being monitored closely. Dylan Cook is the likely fallback but is unproven as a starter. OT remains a tier 2 need with injury-driven uncertainty. If Jones is cleared for training camp, need drops to tier 3. If he misses regular season time, this escalates to tier 1 alongside IOL.

Pittman trade + 3yr/$59M extension fully addresses WR2. Pittsburgh now has DK Metcalf (WR1) and Pittman (WR2) as a legitimate starting pair. WR3/depth is still thin — Calvin Austin III struggled in 2025 and is not a reliable No. 3 — but the top-of-the-lineup need is resolved. WR drops from tier 1 critical to tier 3 depth/future need.

Jamel Dean (3yr/$36.75M) fully resolves the CB2 hole left by James Pierre. Dean is a proven starter with 3 INTs in 14 starts in 2025. Paired with Joey Porter Jr. (CB1) and Asante Samuel Jr. (depth/slot), the secondary is now one of the team's strengths. Jalen Ramsey also remains under contract. CB drops from tier 2 to tier 4 — at most a developmental or late-round add.

Rico Dowdle (2yr/$12.25M) addresses the volume and rushing production lost with Gainwell, but not the pass-catching role. Gainwell had 73 receptions in 2025; Dowdle is primarily a runner. Jaylen Warren + Dowdle + Kaleb Johnson form a functional committee but the receiving back role is not fully solved. RB drops from tier 2 to tier 3 — a moderate need, primarily for a pass-catching back or receiving-capable RB2.
Heyward re-signed (2yr/$32.25M through 2027), Benton and Harmon are young building blocks, and Otomewo provides rotational depth. IDL remains a strength — no change needed.
Patrick Queen and Payton Wilson are a strong starting pair. Holcomb re-signing preserves depth. No change to LB need tier.

Jonnu Smith released, freeing cap space but leaving thin TE depth. Pat Freiermuth and Darnell Washington are a quality starting duo. Connor Heyward (TE/FB hybrid) is a free agent whose status is unresolved. No TE signing has been made. TE3/depth remains a need — unchanged at tier 3. Will likely be addressed late in free agency or via mid-round draft pick.

Safety was already flagged as a need pre-FA. Jalen Ramsey is the only proven safety under contract. DeShon Elliott is returning from a season-ending knee injury and on an expiring deal. No safety signing has been made in this wave of free agency. Minkah Fitzpatrick was traded to the Jets (not available to Pittsburgh). Safety remains a tier 2 need that has not been addressed in FA.

key_additions.headline: Reliable WR2 (65+ catches in 5 straight seasons) acquired via trade to pair with DK Metcalf
Top CB free agent fills CB2 void alongside Joey Porter Jr. with 3 INTs in 14 starts in 2025
Back-to-back 1,000-yard rusher replaces Gainwell with McCarthy-scheme familiarity from Dallas days
2nd-team All-Pro DT locked up through 2027 on largest contract ever for an NFL defender age 36+
Re-signed returning corner provides slot depth in now-loaded secondary
Re-signed as LB3 depth/special teams behind Queen and Wilson
Rotational DL retained on minimum-level deal to maintain interior depth
Veteran Australian punter returns to Pittsburgh on one-year deal to compete for starting job

key_losses.headline: Pro Bowl LG and top pass-blocking guard departs, leaving Pittsburgh's biggest remaining roster hole at left guard
2025 team MVP (1,023 scrimmage yards, 73 rec, 8 TDs) and Rodgers' top check-down option departs to Tampa Bay
Breakout CB2 starter heads to Minnesota; departure largely covered by Jamel Dean signing
Disappointing one-year stint ends with release; frees cap space, affirms Freiermuth/Washington as TE starters
42-year-old starter has not committed to returning; QB situation is team's most unresolved and critical issue

notes: Aaron Rodgers is officially a UFA as of March 11 and has not signed anywhere. The Steelers are widely assumed to be his most likely landing spot but no deal has been announced. This output treats him as a departure/open need.
Otomewo contract terms not yet publicly reported; estimated near minimum based on RFA tender context (~$1.5-2M). Will update when official terms released.
Johnston contract terms not yet publicly reported; estimated ~$1-2M based on deal description as cheap/one-year. Will update when official terms released.
Jamel Dean and Pittman guaranteed money figures not yet fully reported. Figures noted as TBD will be updated once OverTheCap/Spotrac confirm full structure.
Broderick Jones (LT) is under contract but underwent spinal fusion surgery; his Week 1 availability is uncertain. OT need assessment reflects this medical uncertainty.
Steelers were awarded four compensatory picks for the 2026 NFL Draft, giving them a projected 12-pick draft class — significant draft capital to address IOL, QB, OT, S, and TE needs.
Cap space post-FA based on OverTheCap real-time figure of ~$14.25M as of March 11, 2026 (pre-league-year official open); figure reflects all reported signings and the Heyward restructure.
